task_completion.description: The dwanko_run.tnz scene is loaded in OpenToonz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application is open, and the title bar, xsheet, or level strip indicates that 'dwanko_run' is loaded. The character animation should be visible in the main viewer.

task_completion.description: The background image is imported into the OpenToonz scene.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The xsheet or timeline shows a column containing 'scene_background', or the background image (a Studio Ghibli scene) is visible in the main viewer.

task_completion.description: The character animation is composited over the background image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main viewer in OpenToonz shows the character animation layered on top of the imported background image.

task_completion.description: The output settings are configured for a PNG sequence to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Output Settings' window in OpenToonz is open, showing the 'Save In' path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/composite_frames/' (or similar) and the file format set to 'png'.

task_completion.description: The composite frames are rendered to the output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows multiple PNG files in the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/composite_frames/' directory, or an OpenToonz render progress window is shown completing.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenToonz GUI to perform the compositing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenToonz interface being actively used (e.g., menus open, xsheet modified, viewer updated), rather than terminal commands performing the work.

integrity.description: The frames were rendered using OpenToonz.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz render settings or render progress bar is visible, indicating the software itself generated the output frames.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands like ImageMagick or FFmpeg to bypass the OpenToonz GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows, if visible, do not contain commands like 'convert', 'ffmpeg', or python scripts that composite the images directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source scene is 'dwanko_run.tnz'. The background image is 'scene_background.jpg' (a Studio Ghibli scene). The expected output is a PNG frame sequence of at least 20 frames at 1920x1080 resolution, saved to /home/ga/OpenToonz/output/composite_frames/.
